info.title: devops
info.description: ﻿devops is a command line tool designed to interact with the Azure DevOps and Azure DevOps Server APIs

options.name: --service-url
options.description:
URL to the service you will connect to, e.g.
https://dev.azure.com/organization.

options.name: --non-interactive
options.description: When set, the tool will run in non interactive mode.

options.name: --agent-queue-id
options.description:
Agent queue id
Default value is: 0.

options.name: --output-file
options.description:
File to export the agent queue details. If this value is
not provided the output will be the console.

options.description:

options.description: When set, the tool will run in non interactive mode.

options.description: Get Agent Queues with names starting with searchText.

Build definition id. if this value is not
provided the import command will create a new
build definition group otherwise it will attempt
to update the build definition with the provided
identifier.
Default value is: 0.

commands.name: configure
commands.description: Configure the DevOps CLI or view the configuration.

options.name: --service-url
options.description:
Set the default service url (Azure DevOps Organization) to use
if none is provided.

options.name: --project
options.description: Set the default project name to use if none is provided.

commands.name: create
commands.description: Request (create) a release given a release definition.

commands.name: defaults
commands.description: Configure default values for common parameters (i.e. serviceUrl, project).

commands.description: Commands for work with git repositories is Azure DevOps.

https://dev.azure.com/organization.

options.name: --non-interactive

https://dev.azure.com/organization.

options.name: --non-interactive
options.description: When set, the tool will run in non interactive mode.

options.name: --repository
options.description: The id or friendly name of the repository.

options.name: --searchCriteria-version
options.description:
Version string identifier (name of tag/branch,
SHA1 of commit)

options.name: --top
options.description:
Number of commits to get. Default is 50.
Default value is: 0.

options.name: --service-url
options.description:
URL to the service you will connect to, e.g.
https://dev.azure.com/organization.

options.description:

options.name: --service-url
options.description:
URL to the service you will connect to, e.g.
https://dev.azure.com/organization.

options.name: --non-interactive
options.description: When set, the tool will run in non interactive mode.

options.name: --repository
options.description: The id or friendly name of the repository.

options.name: --top
options.description:
Number of pull requests to get.
Default value is: 100.

options.name: --skip
options.description:
Number of pull requests to skip.
Default value is: 0.

options.name: --timerange-type
options.description:
The type of time range on which the pull requests are
filtered.
Allowed values are: Created, Closed.
Default value is: Created.

options.name: --min-time
options.description:
If specified, filters pull requests that created/closed
after this date based on the queryTimeRangeType specified.

options.name: --max-time
options.description:
If specified, filters pull requests that created/closed
before this date based on the queryTimeRangeType specified.

options.name: --status
options.description:
The status of the pull request.
Allowed values are: NotSet, Active, Completed, Abandoned,
All.
Default value is: NotSet.

options.name: --export-all
options.description:
If specified, retrieves and exports all available pull
requests.

options.name: --output-file
options.description:
File to export the pullrequest list. If this value is not
provided the output will be the console. If --export-all is
set this --output-file is required.

commands.name: identity
commands.description: Resolve legacy identity information for use with older APIs such as the Security APIs.

options.name: --service-url
options.description:
URL to the service you will connect to, e.g.
https://dev.azure.com/organization.

options.name: --non-interactive
options.description: When set, the tool will run in non interactive mode.

options.name: --identity-ids
options.description:
Specify a non-default scope (collection, project) to
search for groups.

options.name: --query-membership
options.description:
The membership information to include with the identities.
Values can be None for no membership data or Direct to
include the groups that the identity is a member of and
the identities that are a member of this identity (groups
only).
Allowed values are: None, Direct, Expanded, ExpandedDown,
ExpandedUp.
Default value is: None.

options.name: --search-filter
options.description:
The type of search to perform. Values can be AccountName
(domainlias), DisplayName, MailAddress, General (display
name, account name, or unique name), or LocalGroupName
(only search Azure Devops groups).

options.name: --filter-value
options.description: The search value, as specified by the searchFilter.

options.name: --service-url
options.description:
URL to the service you will connect to, e.g.
https://dev.azure.com/organization.

options.name: --non-interactive
options.description: When set, the tool will run in non interactive mode.

options.name: --status
options.description:
Filter approvals with this status. Default is 'pending'.
(valid values here
https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/azure/devops/release/approvals/list?view=azure-devops-rest-5.1#approvalstatus)

options.name: --release-id
options.description:
Release id. You can specify this multiple times to include
multiple releases in the filter

provided the output will be the console.
